evidence.snippet:
ET 15
=====
Shortening distances to save lives

CASTA
=====
Modular UAV for precision cargo missions

items.notes: ET15 (eVTOL/hybrid-VTOL cargo UAV) and CASTA (multirotor cargo UAV) are complete unmanned aircraft platforms designed and manufactured by Eliot.

evidence.snippet: The Flight Termination System (FTS) is a safety solution designed to enhance the operational security of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs).

items.notes: FTS (Flight Termination System) is a standalone hardware product sold for integration into any UAV, EASA-compliant and compatible with MOC Light-UAS.2511.01.

evidence.snippet: Our software is designed to ensure compliance, efficiency, and seamless management of your drone fleet.

items.notes: UATracker is a drone fleet management software platform covering fleet inventory, crew management, mission planning, maintenance scheduling, and real-time monitoring.

value.address: R. Luís Adelino Fonseca, Lt 1A, Pact - Pq do Alentejo Ciencia Tecnologia, Herdade da Barba Rala 7005-841 Évora

evidence.snippet: Eliot, with registered address at R. Luís Adelino Fonseca, Lt 1A, Pact - Pq do Alentejo Ciencia Tecnologia, Herdade da Barba Rala 7005-841 Évora, collects the personal data necessary for contact and communication with the users of its site, such as:

headquarters.notes: Registered address from privacy policy. Contact page also lists 14 Tottenham Road, London, England. News consistently references Portugal as home country; company received Portuguese government PRR funding.

evidence.snippet: Our flying platform is designed to revolutionize air mobility, addressing critical needs in medical, emergency, and defense logistics.
The CASTA platform is a cutting-edge multirotor system designed to test and validate enabling technologies for Advanced Air Mobility (AAM).

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Eliot designs and manufactures complete UAV platforms (ET15, CASTA). Also sells a standalone FTS subsystem and UATracker fleet management software, but the primary business is complete-platform development.

items.name: ET15

evidence.snippet:
ET 15
=====
Shortening distances to save lives

items.notes: 65 kg MTOW, 15 kg / 55 L payload, 100 km range, 120 km/h cruise. Targets medical logistics, emergency response, defense logistics. Fully electric with hybrid option.

items.name: CASTA

evidence.snippet:
CASTA
=====
Modular UAV for precision cargo missions

items.notes: 45 kg MTOW, 15 kg payload, 30 km range, 35 km/h cruise. Quadcopter (octocopter config available). Used for AAM tech validation and cargo missions.

items.name: FTS

evidence.snippet:
FTS
===
Enabling technology for urban and semi-urban operations

items.notes: Ground unit + air unit. EASA compliant; compatible with MOC Light-UAS.2511.01. Customisation available. Cut-off up to 50 A / 12S.

items.name: UATracker

evidence.snippet:
UATracker
=========
The Brain Behind the Fleet

items.notes: Agnostic platform; covers fleet inventory, maintenance scheduling, crew/pilot certification, mission planning, real-time monitoring. Seamless DJI Enterprise integration.

ndaa.notes: No NDAA Section 848/889 mention anywhere in the corpus. Company is Portuguese; NDAA compliance is US-specific and not addressed on site.

blue_uas.notes: No Blue UAS / Blue sUAS mention anywhere in the corpus.

evidence.snippet: The CASTA platform is a cutting-edge multirotor system designed to test and validate enabling technologies for Advanced Air Mobility (AAM).

readiness.notes: ET15 completed its first unmanned test flight at Hospital São João in Oct 2025; CASTA is explicitly described as a platform to 'test and validate enabling technologies.' Company received a PEL Startup Award in 2025. FTS and UATracker may be at a higher readiness level.

unresolved_questions: London address on contact page vs. Évora registered address — likely a UK commercial office separate from registered HQ in Portugal.

fetch_requests.reason: Would confirm company formation, founding story, team location, and any additional office addresses to resolve London vs. Évora HQ ambiguity.

tagline: Portuguese OEM producing electric fixed-wing (ET15) and modular multirotor (CASTA) cargo UAVs with 15 kg payload, plus a MOC Light-compliant flight termination system and fleet management software. Currently at prototype readiness. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown. Public evidence limited; positioning targets logistics and defense but supplier chain visibility and US regulatory pathway remain unclear.
